name: Mascoma Bank
description_short: Community-focused bank serving Northern New England since 1899, operating as a Certified B Corporation committed to stakeholder benefit over shareholder profit.
description_long:
Mascoma Bank was established in 1899 to serve the financial needs of Northern New England communities. The bank has evolved its mission to explicitly prioritize community benefit, becoming a Certified B Corporation® that explicitly states it was "established to help our communities and customers, not to benefit investors." This foundational commitment to place-based banking distinguishes the institution in a consolidating banking industry.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail and commercial banking services including personal deposit accounts, loans and mortgages, digital banking platforms, business deposit accounts, business loans, wealth management, and community development financing. According to 2024 data on their website, they issued 236 first mortgages to families and individuals. They also operate Mascoma Community Development, a division focused on community development investments that has been awarded $395 million in New Markets Tax Credits since 2014.

Mascoma's distinguishing characteristics include: B Corporation certification requiring legal commitment to stakeholder benefit; 50% female board representation; a philanthropic commitment allocating 10.06% of budgeted pre-tax income to giving; $11.4 million in nonprofit donations over five years; BankOn certification from the Cities for Financial Empowerment Fund ensuring safe and affordable account access; and an employee satisfaction metric showing 90% of staff feel their manager or someone at work cares about them as a person. The bank emphasizes fraud prevention and financial wellness education.

As a regional community bank, Mascoma operates within the constraints typical of smaller institutions—likely more limited product complexity and geographic reach than national banks, and potentially higher fees in some categories to offset operational costs. While their B Corporation status and community commitment are genuine and verifiable, customers should still evaluate specific account terms, rates, and loan products against competitors. The bank's primary strength lies in its documented institutional commitment to community benefit rather than in product innovation or competitive pricing.
services: Mortgages and home loans (236 issued in 2024)
Community development financing and New Markets Tax Credit investments
pros: Certified B Corporation® with legal obligation to consider all stakeholders, not just shareholders
Allocates 10.06% of budgeted pre-tax income to philanthropic giving annually
BankOn Certified ensuring access to safe and affordable accounts for underserved populations
50% female board representation demonstrating governance diversity commitment
$395 million in New Markets Tax Credits awarded to Mascoma Community Development since 2014
90% of employees report feeling cared for by management, indicating strong workplace culture
Community-focused since 1899 with documented $11.4 million nonprofit support over 5 years
cons: As a regional bank, likely offers more limited product variety compared to national competitors
Geographic focus on Northern New England may limit accessibility for customers outside region
No specific information provided on deposit rates, loan terms, or fee structures for rate/price comparison
Smaller institution scale may mean fewer branch locations and ATM networks than major banks
Limited digital innovation details shared beyond mention of 'Digital Banking' offering
best_for: Northern New England residents seeking banking aligned with community-focused values
Customers prioritizing ethical banking and institutional commitment to social benefit
Underbanked or underserved populations eligible for BankOn-certified affordable accounts
Small business owners and nonprofits seeking community development lending partnerships
diagnosis: Mascoma Bank is best for Northern New England residents and businesses seeking community-oriented banking with transparent commitment to stakeholder benefit over profit maximization, verified through B Corporation certification and substantial philanthropic allocation. The primary caveat is that community mission, while documented and genuine, should not substitute for evaluating specific account rates, fees, and loan terms against regional and national competitors to ensure competitive value.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
